steps.title: Read the circle
steps.content:
Compare the equation with the standard circle form.
$$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$
For $(x+5)^2+(y-14)^2=144$, the center is $(-5,14)$ and the radius is $12$.

steps.title: Find possible x-values
steps.content:
The $x$-coordinate of any point on the circle must be within $12$ units of the center's $x$-coordinate.
$$-5-12\le a\le -5+12$$
$$-17\le a\le 7$$

steps.title: Test the choices
steps.content: Choice A is less than $-17$, while choices C and D are greater than $7$. The only listed value in the possible range is $-12$. The answer is B.
